NCT03518086.name: LUCENT-1
NCT03518086.group: Moderate-severe UC, mirikizumab 300 mg IV wk 0/4/8 vs placebo induction (wk 12, clinical remission)
NCT03518086.snippet: Source: DHaens G et al. N Engl J Med 2023;388:2444-2455 (LUCENT-1).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 1,281 adults with moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis (modified Mayo score 4-9) with inadequate response, loss of response, or intolerance to conventional or biologic therapy randomized 3:1 double-blind to mirikizumab 300 mg IV at weeks 0, 4, 8 (n=868) or matching placebo (n=294) for 12-week induction.

evidence.label: Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 12
evidence.text: Clinical remission at week 12 (modified Mayo score <=2 with each subscore <=1 and rectal bleeding subscore =0): mirikizumab 210/868 (24.2%) vs placebo 39/294 (13.3%); odds ratio 2.08 (95% CI 1.44 to 3.02; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met.

evidence.label: Secondary - Endoscopic Remission and Clinical Response
evidence.text: Endoscopic remission (Mayo endoscopic subscore <=1) at wk 12: mirikizumab 317/868 (36.5%) vs placebo 63/294 (21.4%); OR 2.05 (95% CI 1.50-2.81). Clinical response: 65% vs 43%.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central IRT stratified by biologic-failure and baseline disease activity. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo IV infusions. D3 LOW - 93% completion. D4 LOW - blinded central endoscopy. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Diagnosis of UC for at least 3 months prior to baseline.~* Confirmed diagnosis of moderately or severely active UC, as assessed by the modified Mayo score (MMS).~* Demonstrated an inadequate response to, a loss of response to, or an intolerance to conventional or to biologic therapy for UC.~* If female, must meet the contraception requirements.~Exclusion Criteria:~* Participants with a current diagnosis of Crohn's disease or inflammatory bowel disease-unclassified (indeter...

NCT03524092.name: LUCENT-2
NCT03524092.group: LUCENT-1 responders, mirikizumab 200 mg SC Q4W vs placebo maintenance (wk 40, clinical remission)
NCT03524092.snippet: Source: DHaens G et al. N Engl J Med 2023;388:2444-2455 (LUCENT-2).

evidence.label: Enrollment
evidence.text: 544 adults who had achieved clinical response to mirikizumab IV induction (LUCENT-1 week 12 responders) re-randomized 2:1 double-blind to maintenance with mirikizumab 200 mg SC every 4 weeks (n=365) or matching placebo (n=179) through week 40 of maintenance (wk 52 of programme).

evidence.label: Primary - Clinical Remission at Week 40
evidence.text: Clinical remission at week 40: mirikizumab 182/365 (49.9%) vs placebo 45/179 (25.1%); odds ratio 2.97 (95% CI 2.01 to 4.38; P<0.001). Primary endpoint met. Durable remission at 52 weeks.

evidence.label: Secondary - Endoscopic + Steroid-Free Remission
evidence.text: Endoscopic remission at wk 40: mirikizumab 159/365 (43.6%) vs placebo 39/179 (21.8%); OR 2.80 (95% CI 1.85-4.24). Corticosteroid-free remission rate higher with mirikizumab.

evidence.label: Risk of Bias (RoB 2.0)
evidence.text: D1 LOW - central re-randomization stratified by LUCENT-1 induction response type. D2 LOW - double-blind matched placebo SC. D3 LOW - 87% completion at wk 40. D4 LOW - blinded central endoscopy. D5 LOW - SAP locked.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. title: RapidMeta Gastroenterology | Mirikizumab for Moderate-to-Severe Ulcerative Colitis v1.1
